lines.english: Ongkaar saves the world through the Shabad.
Ongkaar saves the Gurmukhs.
The Universal, Imperishable Creator Lord is the essence of the three worlds. ||1||
Listen to the Message of the Universal, Imperishable Creator Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਲਿਖੁ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਗੋਪਾਲਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: likh raam naam guramukh gopaalaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: As Gurmukh, write only the Name of the Lord, the Lord of the World. ||1||Pause||
Listen, O Pandit, O religious scholar, why are you writing about worldly debates?

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਸੈ ਸਭੁ ਜਗੁ ਸਹਜਿ ਉਪਾਇਆ ਤੀਨਿ ਭਵਨ ਇਕ ਜੋਤੀ ॥
lines.translit: sasai sabh jag sehaj upaaeaa teen bhavan ik jotee |
lines.english: Sassa: He created the entire universe with ease; His One Light pervades the three worlds.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਵਸਤੁ ਪਰਾਪਤਿ ਹੋਵੈ ਚੁਣਿ ਲੈ ਮਾਣਕ ਮੋਤੀ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh vasat paraapat hovai chun lai maanak motee |
lines.english: Become Gurmukh, and obtain the real thing; gather the gems and pearls.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਮਝੈ ਸੂਝੈ ਪੜਿ ਪੜਿ ਬੂਝੈ ਅੰਤਿ ਨਿਰੰਤਰਿ ਸਾਚਾ ॥
lines.translit: samajhai soojhai parr parr boojhai ant nirantar saachaa |
lines.english: If one understands, realizes and comprehends what he reads and studies, in the end he shall realize that the True Lord dwells deep within his nucleus.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਦੇਖੈ ਸਾਚੁ ਸਮਾਲੇ ਬਿਨੁ ਸਾਚੇ ਜਗੁ ਕਾਚਾ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: guramukh dekhai saach samaale bin saache jag kaachaa |2|
lines.english: The Gurmukh sees and contemplates the True Lord; without the True Lord, the world is false. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਧਧੈ ਧਰਮੁ ਧਰੇ ਧਰਮਾ ਪੁਰਿ ਗੁਣਕਾਰੀ ਮਨੁ ਧੀਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: dhadhai dharam dhare dharamaa pur gunakaaree man dheeraa |
lines.english: Dhadha: Those who enshrine Dharmic faith and dwell in the City of Dharma are worthy; their minds are steadfast and stable.

lines.gurmukhi: ਧਧੈ ਧੂਲਿ ਪੜੈ ਮੁਖਿ ਮਸਤਕਿ ਕੰਚਨ ਭਏ ਮਨੂਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: dhadhai dhool parrai mukh masatak kanchan bhe manooraa |
lines.english: Dhadha: If the dust of their feet touches one's face and forehead, he is transformed from iron into gold.

lines.gurmukhi: ਧਨੁ ਧਰਣੀਧਰੁ ਆਪਿ ਅਜੋਨੀ ਤੋਲਿ ਬੋਲਿ ਸਚੁ ਪੂਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: dhan dharaneedhar aap ajonee tol bol sach pooraa |
lines.english: Blessed is the Support of the Earth; He Himself is not born; His measure and speech are perfect and True.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਤੇ ਕੀ ਮਿਤਿ ਕਰਤਾ ਜਾਣੈ ਕੈ ਜਾਣੈ ਗੁਰੁ ਸੂਰਾ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: karate kee mit karataa jaanai kai jaanai gur sooraa |3|
lines.english: Only the Creator Himself knows His own extent; He alone knows the Brave Guru. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਙਿਆਨੁ ਗਵਾਇਆ ਦੂਜਾ ਭਾਇਆ ਗਰਬਿ ਗਲੇ ਬਿਖੁ ਖਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: ngiaan gavaaeaa doojaa bhaaeaa garab gale bikh khaaeaa |
lines.english: In love with duality, spiritual wisdom is lost; the mortal rots away in pride, and eats poison.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਰਸੁ ਗੀਤ ਬਾਦ ਨਹੀ ਭਾਵੈ ਸੁਣੀਐ ਗਹਿਰ ਗੰਭੀਰੁ ਗਵਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: gur ras geet baad nahee bhaavai suneeai gahir ganbheer gavaaeaa |
lines.english: He thinks that the sublime essence of the Guru's song is useless, and he does not like to hear it. He loses the profound, unfathomable Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਿ ਸਚੁ ਕਹਿਆ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਲਹਿਆ ਮਨਿ ਤਨਿ ਸਾਚੁ ਸੁਖਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: gur sach kahiaa amrit lahiaa man tan saach sukhaaeaa |
lines.english: Through the Guru's Words of Truth, the Ambrosial Nectar is obtained, and the mind and body find joy in the True Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਆਪੇ ਦੇਵੈ ਆਪੇ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਪੀਆਇਆ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: aape guramukh aape devai aape amrit peeaeaa |4|
lines.english: He Himself is the Gurmukh, and He Himself bestows the Ambrosial Nectar; He Himself leads us to drink it in. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਕੋ ਏਕੁ ਕਹੈ ਸਭੁ ਕੋਈ ਹਉਮੈ ਗਰਬੁ ਵਿਆਪੈ ॥
lines.translit: eko ek kahai sabh koee haumai garab viaapai |
lines.english: Everyone says that God is the One and only, but they are engrossed in egotism and pride.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਰਿ ਬਾਹਰਿ ਏਕੁ ਪਛਾਣੈ ਇਉ ਘਰੁ ਮਹਲੁ ਸਿਞਾਪੈ ॥
lines.translit: antar baahar ek pachhaanai iau ghar mehal siyaapai |
lines.english: Realize that the One God is inside and outside; understand this, that the Mansion of His Presence is within the home of your heart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਨੇੜੈ ਹਰਿ ਦੂਰਿ ਨ ਜਾਣਹੁ ਏਕੋ ਸ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਸਬਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: prabh nerrai har door na jaanahu eko srisatt sabaaee |
lines.english: God is near at hand; do not think that God is far away. The One Lord permeates the entire universe.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਸੁ ਕਰਤੇ ਕਉ ਕਿਉ ਗਹਿ ਰਾਖਉ ਅਫਰਿਓ ਤੁਲਿਓ ਨ ਜਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: eis karate kau kiau geh raakhau afario tulio na jaaee |
lines.english: How can you keep the Creator under your control? He cannot be seized or measured.

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਕੰਕਾਰੁ ਅਵਰੁ ਨਹੀ ਦੂਜਾ ਨਾਨਕ ਏਕੁ ਸਮਾਈ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: ekankaar avar nahee doojaa naanak ek samaaee |5|
lines.english: There in One Universal Creator Lord; there is no other at all. O Nanak, merge into the One Lord. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਇਆ ਕੇ ਦੇਵਾਨੇ ਪ੍ਰਾਣੀ ਝੂਠਿ ਠਗਉਰੀ ਪਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: maaeaa ke devaane praanee jhootth tthgauree paaee |
lines.english: Maya has made the mortal insane; she has administered the poisonous drug of falsehood.

lines.gurmukhi: ਲਬਿ ਲੋਭਿ ਮੁਹਤਾਜਿ ਵਿਗੂਤੇ ਇਬ ਤਬ ਫਿਰਿ ਪਛੁਤਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: lab lobh muhataaj vigoote ib tab fir pachhutaaee |
lines.english: Addicted to greed and avarice, the mortal is ruined, and then later, he regrets and repents.
The One Lord is in all actions, colors and forms.

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਕੁ ਸਰੇਵੈ ਤਾ ਗਤਿ ਮਿਤਿ ਪਾਵੈ ਆਵਣੁ ਜਾਣੁ ਰਹਾਈ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: ek sarevai taa gat mit paavai aavan jaan rahaaee |6|
lines.english: So serve the One Lord, and attain the state of Salvation; your comings and goings shall cease. ||6||
The One Soul wanders through the three worlds.
He manifests in many shapes through wind, water and fire.
One who understands and comprehends the One Lord is honored.
How rare are those who, as Gurmukh, attain the One Lord.
One who gathers in spiritual wisdom and meditation, dwells in the state of balance.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸ ਨੋ ਦੇਇ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਤੇ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: jis no dee kirapaa te sukh paae |
lines.english: They alone find peace, whom the Lord blesses with His Grace.
His Light illuminates the ocean and the earth.
Throughout the three worlds, is the Guru, the Lord of the World.
In the Gurdwara, the Guru's Door, they speak and hear of the Lord. ||7||
The Lord reveals His various forms;
The clouds hang low, and the rain is pouring down.
granting His Grace, He enters the home of the heart.
One who knows the mystery of the One God,
When the sun rises, the demons are slain;
The Lord embellishes and exalts with the Sublime Word of the Shabad.
is Himself the Creator, Himself the Divine Lord. ||8||
the mortal looks upwards, and contemplates the Shabad.
He Himself acts, speaks and listens.
The Lord is beyond the beginning and the end, beyond the three worlds.
